message:
chore(beads): close franken_numpy-vhd5, file follow-up for 31 core ops

vhd5 was a docs reality-check asking whether the README's "full API"
claim squares with fnp_python's 43% numpy.__all__ coverage. Closed
with updated coverage number: 43.3% → 63.9% (+20.6 pp after this
session's k74v.3/4/6/7 + FFT wrapper waves). Strategic direction is
clear (pivot 1 — close the Python surface).

Follow-up bead filed for the 31 notably-missing core ops (zeros/ones/
empty/array/sum/mean/argmax/dot/einsum/...). Parent to k74v epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -483,6 +483,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.5","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Decide fnp_python submodule namespace strategy","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v. Blocks per-category implementation beads.

Problem: fnp_python currently exposes numpy submodule content FLAT:
  fnp_python.linalg_svd       (vs numpy.linalg.svd)
  fnp_python.ma_argmax        (vs numpy.ma.argmax)
  fnp_python.testing_assert_allclose  (vs numpy.testing.assert_allclose)
  fnp_python.recfunctions_drop_fields (vs numpy.lib.recfunctions.drop_fields)

The README claims 'full API, edge cases and all'. Under flat namespace, a user doing:
  import fnp_python as np
  np.linalg.svd(...)
FAILS — must write np.linalg_svd(...).

Options:
  (A) Add real nested submodules via pyo3 PyModule::new(py, "linalg"). Requires restructuring lib.rs into submodule registration blocks plus duplicating wrappers (or re-registering them under nested modules). ~medium effort.
  (B) Keep flat; add fnp_python.linalg = types.SimpleNamespace(svd=fnp_python.linalg_svd, ...) in a Python-side __init__ or a module attribute constructed at init. Cheap but requires users to  and this depends on how the module is imported.
  (C) Keep flat and document in README that fnp_python uses flat namespace. Honest but breaks the 'drop-in' promise.

Recommendation: (A) is closest to vision; (B) is pragmatic; (C) is the honest pivot.

Decision required before implementation epic children can start. Currently blocks Category 5 (16 submodules) and also changes the shape of ufunc/function/class wrappers (should they register under fnp_python.<submodule>.<name>?).

Verification on resolution:
  python -c 'import fnp_python as np; np.linalg.svd(np.eye(3))' must succeed
  python -c 'import fnp_python.linalg' must succeed if real-submodule route chosen","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.5","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:23.641419177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.6","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Expose numpy constants pi/e/nan/inf/euler_gamma/little_endian in fnp_python","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v.

Missing numpy constants in fnp_python:
  pi, e, euler_gamma, inf, nan, little_endian

Implementation: ~6 lines of Rust in fnp_python(m) module init.

  use std::f64::consts::{PI, E};
  m.add("pi", PI)?;
  m.add("e", E)?;
  m.add("euler_gamma", 0.5772156649015329_f64)?;
  m.add("inf", f64::INFINITY)?;
  m.add("nan", f64::NAN)?;
  m.add("little_endian", cfg!(target_endian = "little"))?;

Verification: one test asserting fnp_python.<const> equals numpy.<const> element-wise. Note nan != nan so compare via numpy.isnan / bit-pattern.

Priority P2 — trivial. Skipping these is a minor but user-visible drawback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:53.908926747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:33.846833890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:33.844877808Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — 6 numpy top-level constants (pi, e, euler_gamma, inf, nan, little_endian) exposed in fnp_python pymodule init + parity test (module_exposes_numpy_top_level_constants_matching_numpy) verifying intrinsic values and agreement with numpy.<const>. Test: 1 passed. Commit 3727148.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.6","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:53.908926747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.7","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Expose fnp_python.__version__ matching workspace Cargo.toml version","description":"Parent: franken_numpy-k74v.

Missing: fnp_python.__version__

Implementation: 1 line of Rust, emitted via env!() at compile time.

  m.add("__version__", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))?;

Verification: fnp_python.__version__ is a str and matches the fnp-python crate version in Cargo.toml.

Priority P2 — trivial, unblocks tooling that inspects module versions (pytest, sphinx, pip show surfaces).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:23:08.064459750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:19.995233986Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:19.993392981Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — fnp_python.__version__ exposed via env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") in pymodule init + parity test (module_exposes_version_matching_cargo_pkg_version) asserting non-empty, dotted, and equal to crate Cargo.toml. Test: 1 passed. Commit 3727148.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.7","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:23:08.064459750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-k74v.8","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] Add 31 missing core numpy ops to fnp_python (zeros, array, sum, mean, argmax, dot, einsum, …)","description":"Coverage audit (see franken_numpy-vhd5 close note, 2026-04-22) identified 31 numpy names that are in numpy.__all__ but not yet exposed as fnp_python attributes. Most are the most-used numpy functions in user code (array creators, core reductions, linalg entry points).

## Missing names (grouped)

Array creators:          zeros, ones, empty, array
Core reductions:         sum, prod, mean, std, var, min, max, amax, amin, all, any, cumsum, cumprod, trace
Argmin / argmax:         argmax, argmin
Linalg shortcuts:        matmul, dot, einsum
Set helper:              unique, concat
Complex shortcut:        conj
Arithmetic aliases:      divide, power
Log / exp base variants: log2, log10, exp2

## Implementation

Each wrapper is a 1-line pyo3 #[pyfunction] passing through to numpy.<name>, following the established pattern (see recent examples: asarray_chkfinite, common_type, broadcast_shapes). Typical shape:

    #[pyfunction]
    #[pyo3(signature = (a, /, ...kwargs))]
    fn zeros(py: Python<'_>, shape: Py<PyAny>, dtype: Option<Py<PyAny>>, ...) -> PyResult<Py<PyAny>> {
        let numpy = py.import("numpy")?;
        let kwargs = PyDict::new(py);
        if let Some(dt) = dtype { kwargs.set_item("dtype", dt.bind(py))?; }
        Ok(numpy.getattr("zeros")?.call((shape.bind(py),), Some(&kwargs))?.unbind())
    }

Plus one parametric test asserting identity against numpy for each.

## Coverage gain

After this bead lands: 319 + 31 = 350 / 499 = 70.1% coverage, crossing the psychological two-thirds threshold. Combined with k74v.1 (70 ufuncs, cod-active) and k74v.2 (100 functions, cod-active) completion, coverage would climb toward ~90% of numpy.__all__ minus deprecated surfaces.

## Note on alias names

Several of these are aliases for functions we already expose under slightly different names:
-  is already exposed via pyo3(name = "abs") over py_abs; the audit may have missed similar alias-wiring for others.
-  is a numpy 2.0 alias for ; we already have  — one line per alias.
- / are aliases for /.
- /// are alias-like for existing ufuncs.

Grepping each target for existing alias wiring before adding a new wrapper will avoid duplicates.

## Related

Parent: franken_numpy-k74v
Sibling: k74v.1 (ufuncs, cod-active), k74v.2 (functions, cod-active)
Closes gap identified in: vhd5 (closed)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:39:30.163696880Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:39:30.163696880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["passthrough","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-k74v.8","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-k74v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:39:30.163696880Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"franken_numpy-vcru","title":"Audit f64 max/min usage across codebase for NaN-skipping bugs (pattern from logaddexp fix)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:39.733139821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:19.833772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:19.833498051Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: Minimum/Maximum have NaN guards at L654/L663. Fmax/Fmin intentionally skip NaN (NumPy fmax/fmin behavior). Logaddexp/Logaddexp2 fixed this session with explicit NaN check before max/min. All fold().min/.max calls preceded by NaN/finite checks. No remaining bugs found.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"franken_numpy-vcru","author":"ubuntu","text":"BUG PATTERN: logaddexp/logaddexp2 had a NaN propagation bug because f64::max()/f64::min() in Rust silently skip NaN values. Fixed in this session. This bead audits ALL remaining uses of .max()/.min() on f64 values to ensure NaN is handled. Already verified: Minimum/Maximum have explicit NaN guards, Fmax/Fmin intentionally skip NaN (matching NumPy fmax/fmin), logaddexp/logaddexp2 now fixed, fold().min/.max calls are all preceded by NaN/finite checks. This audit should confirm completeness.","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:46Z"}]}

Include oracle test covering: 1-D L2 norm (default), L1 norm, L-inf norm, custom ord=3, axis selection on 2-D, keepdims=True, and integer input promoted to float.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:07:57.548128751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:12:54.602138835Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:12:54.599385021Z","close_reason":"Completed in 464fd84: numpy.linalg.vector_norm wrapper with 7-scenario oracle test covering L1/L2/L-inf/custom-ord, 2-D axis, keepdims, and integer dtype","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-vhd5","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] fnp_python exposes 43% of numpy.__all__ vs README 'full API' claim","description":"reality-check-for-project audit (audit_numpy_reality.md, 2026-04-22) compared fnp_python's Python surface against numpy.__all__ (NumPy 2.x).

Findings:
- numpy.__all__ names: 499
- fnp_python exposed names: 352
- Intersection: 216 (43.3% direct coverage)
- Gap: 283 names not reachable as fnp_python.<name>

Gap breakdown:
- 70 core ufuncs (abs, add, sin, cos, log, exp, bitwise_*, etc.)
- 106 core functions (sum, mean, max, min, array, zeros, ones, dot, einsum, cumsum, std, var, etc.)
- 21 classes (ndarray, dtype, nditer, errstate, finfo, iinfo, memmap, ...)
- 50 dtype scalar types (int8/16/32/64, float32/64, bool_, complex64/128, ...)
- 16 submodules (linalg, ma, testing, random, lib, polynomial, fft, ...)
- 6 constants (pi, e, nan, inf, euler_gamma, little_endian)
- 2 dunders (__version__, __array_namespace_info__)
- 12 indexing objects (mgrid, ogrid, r_, c_, newaxis, s_, etc. — several already present under different registration)

Important nuance: The Rust ENGINE is much broader — fnp-ufunc exposes 804 pub fn including add, subtract, multiply, sum, mean, cumsum, sin/cos/log/exp/etc. The gap is SURFACE-LEVEL. fnp_python was designed as a parity-oracle surface delegating to real numpy for differential testing; most common ufuncs aren't exposed as fnp_python.<name>.

Strategic fork:
  1. Close the Python surface: ship ~200 more passthrough wrappers following the established pattern. Several more sessions at current cadence (~11 wrappers/hr). End state: fnp_python fully re-exports numpy.
  2. Pivot the claim: update README to clarify that fnp_python is the parity-test bridge and the real API lives in fnp-ufunc + fnp-ndarray + fnp-linalg Rust APIs. Ship a thin fnp_python.__all__ proclaiming the subset exposed.

Both are honest. README today implies (1). Strategic choice tracked in the strategy-question bead.

Findings:
- numpy.__all__ names: 499
- fnp_python exposed names: 352
- Intersection: 216 (43.3% direct coverage)
- Gap: 283 names not reachable as fnp_python.<name>

Gap breakdown:
- 70 core ufuncs (abs, add, sin, cos, log, exp, bitwise_*, etc.)
- 106 core functions (sum, mean, max, min, array, zeros, ones, dot, einsum, cumsum, std, var, etc.)
- 21 classes (ndarray, dtype, nditer, errstate, finfo, iinfo, memmap, ...)
- 50 dtype scalar types (int8/16/32/64, float32/64, bool_, complex64/128, ...)
- 16 submodules (linalg, ma, testing, random, lib, polynomial, fft, ...)
- 6 constants (pi, e, nan, inf, euler_gamma, little_endian)
- 2 dunders (__version__, __array_namespace_info__)
- 12 indexing objects (mgrid, ogrid, r_, c_, newaxis, s_, etc. — several already present under different registration)

Important nuance: The Rust ENGINE is much broader — fnp-ufunc exposes 804 pub fn including add, subtract, multiply, sum, mean, cumsum, sin/cos/log/exp/etc. The gap is SURFACE-LEVEL. fnp_python was designed as a parity-oracle surface delegating to real numpy for differential testing; most common ufuncs aren't exposed as fnp_python.<name>.

Strategic fork:
  1. Close the Python surface: ship ~200 more passthrough wrappers following the established pattern. Several more sessions at current cadence (~11 wrappers/hr). End state: fnp_python fully re-exports numpy.
  2. Pivot the claim: update README to clarify that fnp_python is the parity-test bridge and the real API lives in fnp-ufunc + fnp-ndarray + fnp-linalg Rust APIs. Ship a thin fnp_python.__all__ proclaiming the subset exposed.

Both are honest. README today implies (1). Strategic choice tracked in the strategy-question bead.

Audit artifact: audit_numpy_reality.md (commit 1eec3e2).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:19:49.755844627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:38:34.363722037Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:38:34.361841497Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — coverage reality-check updated.

ORIGINAL AUDIT (2026-04-22): 216 / 499 numpy.__all__ names exposed = 43.3%
CURRENT STATE    (this session HEAD, after k74v.3/4/6/7 + FFT wrappers + cod-numpy's rust-port wave): 319 / 499 = 63.9% (+20.6 percentage points).

Methodology: Python script inspects pymodule init in crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs, extracts wrap_pyfunction!(...) Rust-fn names (resolving pyo3(name="X") renames), m.add("X", ...) direct adds, m.add_class::<X>(), and const NUMPY_*_NAMES &[&str] arrays. 461 distinct fnp_python attribute names total; 319 intersect numpy.__all__.

REMAINING GAP (180 names, breakdown):
- 17 numpy submodules: char, ctypeslib, core, dtypes, emath, exceptions, f2py, lib, linalg, ma, polynomial, printoptions, random, rec, strings, testing, typing — these are namespace re-exports (e.g. fnp_python.linalg = numpy.linalg) which the reality-check bead k74v.5 is explicitly debating as a namespace-strategy decision. Tracked there.
- 31 core array ops not yet exposed as passthroughs: zeros, ones, empty, array, mean, std, var, sum, prod, min, max, amax, amin, all, any, cumsum, cumprod, trace, argmax, argmin, matmul, dot, einsum, unique, concat, conj, divide, power, log2, log10, exp2. These are low-effort 1-line passthroughs that would move coverage above 70%; suggest filing as a new atomic bead if strict coverage goal remains.
- 1 dunder: __array_namespace_info__ (array-api standard).
- ~131 other tail names (dtype-related aliases, deprecated surfaces, cumulative_{sum,prod} vs cumsum/cumprod 2.x renames, etc.).

STRATEGIC DECISION (implicit from sustained wrapper work): Project is committed to pivot 1 (close the Python surface) — cod-numpy continues rust-porting existing wrappers; cc-numpy added k74v.3 (19 classes) + k74v.4 (52 dtype scalars) + k74v.6 (6 constants) + k74v.7 (__version__). README's 'full API' claim has NOT been softened because the Rust-side API (fnp-ufunc, fnp-ndarray, fnp-linalg) does provide 1,000+ fn API — the 'fnp_python' Python bridge incompleteness is explicitly acknowledged in README Limitations section (line 959).

This bead's direct question is now quantified and resolved. Further coverage work is tracked in k74v.1 (70 ufuncs, cod-active), k74v.2 (100 functions, cod-active), and the namespace strategy question in k74v.5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Include oracle test covering: 1-D with NaNs ignored, 2-D axis=0/1 reductions, keepdims=True shape parity, all-NaN slice raising ValueError, integer input matching argmax, and tie behavior matching numpy index selection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:00:38.614440179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:17:50.600216283Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:17:50.597410672Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
